title: Symmetric put/get: no-field put accepts JSON object
description: The old design rejected put() without ?field= with a Backend error. The new design accepts a JSON object string and writes it as the full KV data map, symmetric with get() without ?field= which returns the full map as JSON. This is the right API surface: it lets callers read, modify, and write full secrets without needing to list all fields in the URI. A contributor might suggest reverting this to 'require ?field= for all puts' for simplicity — this would regress legitimate use cases (atomic multi-field updates). The symmetry principle (put=inverse of get, same field semantics) is the justification.
